source: SimClusters- Community-Based Representations for Heterogeneous Recommendations at Twitter

concepts.finding: SimClusters: community detection on Twitter's user-tweet interaction graph. Each tweet/user represented by its community affiliation vector (soft membership across 145K communities). Uses Metropolis-Hastings sampling for scalable community detection at billion-node scale. Cold-start: infer community affiliation from content features. Communities correspond to topics/interests.
concepts.implication: Community-based keyword representation: cluster keywords via community detection on co-occurrence graph. Each keyword has community affiliation vector across intent communities. Community detection naturally discovers intent clusters from graph structure.
concepts.open_question: Does community detection on keyword co-occurrence graphs discover intent clusters that align with JTBD categories? How to determine optimal number of communities?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: SimClusters-style community detection on keyword co-occurrence graphs produces intent clusters with higher internal coherence than k-Means clustering of keyword embeddings.
